description: WORLDS FAIR EDITION VmiTA TT 77 Tl r II j S0 r 4 4i M i Jii VOL VI NO 305 PLAIN STORY OF LAND CONDITIONS Value of Farming Lands Sell and What is Purchable The facts and figures set forth in this Worlds Fair number of the Dally Chieftain are as well known to the local readers as they are to the writer but they are written on the hypothe cs that the paper will be read by sev eral thousand persons who are not so well acquainted with conditions in the Indian Territory and more es pecially that portion of the Indian section of the new southwest The prohibition of Indian t reaties have re served this fairest and richest country to be settled last Until very recently no land could be leased or bought and t he host class of farmers always want to go where they are able to purchase land A plain story of conditions as they actually exist at the present time without frill or furbelow is deemed wiser than any fine writing in sot ting forth the many excellencies of this tine new country The glamour of romance of Indian Legion of poi
